sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Newcastle United have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, but they remain strong at home. Everton are in decent shape with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, showing resilience with no losses in their last 3 games. Both teams have had inconsistent results recently, but Newcastle's attacking output at home gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results have been tight, with a 0-0 draw and a 1-1 draw, indicating closely contested games. Everton have managed to avoid defeat against Newcastle in their last 3 encounters, which could boost their confidence. However, Newcastle's home advantage at St James' Park may tilt the balance.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Newcastle United rank 4th with 66 points and have scored 68 goals (3rd in the league), showcasing their attacking prowess. Everton sit 13th with 45 points and 41 goals scored, struggling offensively but maintaining a solid defense with 44 goals conceded. Newcastle's superior chance creation (106 big chances vs Everton's likely lower figure) suggests they are more likely to dominate.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Newcastle are missing key forward Alexander Isak (23 goals), which could impact their attack, but players like Harvey Barnes and Jacob Murphy remain threats. Everton miss important defenders like James Tarkowski, potentially weakening their backline. Despite absences, Newcastle's depth and home form should help them compensate.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Newcastle's high expected goals (64.09) and strong final third presence (29.68 penalty area entries per game) indicate they will control the game. Everton's tendency for late goals (31.8% in 76-90 mins) could pose a threat if the game remains close. The match is likely to see a moderate number of cards and corners based on recent trends.
